key_insight: ARIA reduces LLM “contextual tunneling” by choosing how to use evidence based on whether a complete causal Process-Structure-Property chain is available.
engineering_relevance: The framework makes AI-assisted materials discovery more trustworthy by combining causal reasoning, analogical transfer, and parametric fallback with auditable traces.
method: Builds a 2,839-relation knowledge graph from peer-reviewed materials papers.
Routes queries through a three-tier cascade: causal reasoning, physics-informed analogy, or explicit parametric fallback.
Adds online literature search to enrich evidence when needed.
evidence_signal: The authors evaluate ARIA on forward prediction and inverse design for 2D materials, reporting improvements over unaugmented and naive KG-augmented baselines.
watch_item: Performance depends on the completeness and quality of retrieved literature evidence, so noisy or missing PSP links may still limit reliability.
paper_title: ARIA: A Causal-Aware Framework for Rescuing LLM Reasoning in Trustworthy Materials Discovery
